A couple months in and Vouch in Ghostty is working extremely well. Our PR quality is up and the rate of PRs has not gone down at all. Getting a vouch is easy, and the minimal barrier to entry easily filters most. Look at this 5min interaction that saved hours of future anguish. https://t.co/ofcnMEq2Nl

NEW paper from Microsoft.

This is an important read. (bookmark it)

The work introduces DELEGATE-52, a benchmark simulating long document-editing workflows across 52 professional domains like coding, crystallography, and music notation.

Across 19 tested models, even frontier https://t.co/Blh5wZqHiv

Here is a crazy use case for GPT-5.5: I uploaded an image I had generated with GPT Images 2.0 and asked GPT-5.5 Pro to create an interactive, beautiful website from it. After 20 minutes, it created this incredibly beautiful site! Used Codex to publish it: https://t.co/SyPte8hKSb

Traditional inference wasn’t built for agentic coding.

Agentic tools make hundreds of API calls per coding session, often with recomputed context, creating bottlenecks that drive up cost per token.

NVIDIA Dynamo rebuilds the stack for agents with: 
→ KV-aware routing

GPT-5.5 raises the ceiling of ambition for what you can do with AI:

Starting a new educational AI image series with ChatGPT Images 2.0 because it generates such clear and well-organized infographics and, importantly, makes almost no errors. For me, it has significantly surpassed Nano Banana for this purpose. Just a remarkable step change! https://t.co/JrPEyvy0aG
These are amazing math and physics explainer images created by ChatGPT Images-2.0! I created a bunch for biology, which I will share soon.

If you're in #ICLR2026 and are interested in chatting about how to get image generation & editing models to do charts, plots, and math equations. rendering, etc., come check out our poster!

acpx 0.6.0 is out. (control codex/claude via agents)

Highlights: Claude system-prompt controls, session pruning, embeddable turn handles, --no-terminal, persistent-session fixes, WSL cwd translation, queue hardening, and clearer error hints. https://t.co/JUyu0tZYuV

GPT-5.5 in Codex is a delight to work with:

- Super sharp with responses
- It understands intent better than any model
- Great "personality"
- Gets lots of stuff done without pausing unnecessarily

It generated this beautiful artifact design.

Huge win for OpenAI. https://t.co/LKGOLvGU5I
At @tryramp, engineers use GPT-5.5 in Codex to test full-stack changes end to end in QA and identify what needs to improve. https://t.co/tJKTLp8bQK

Image models tend to get much more stuck on a particular direction than text models, requiring clearing the context window fairly often.

PerfectSquashBench is my new measure of how image models anchor. The squash remains merely fine after many attempts. https://t.co/W58vdo0oss

History repeats itself 😀 The concept "learning mechanics" is not a new thing but actually has been explored for a very long time. 

It is human nature to think deeper than a blind belief of the scaling laws. 

I have been working on rigorously modeling training dynamics of deep

I'm giving three workshop talks tomorrow (Sunday) at ICLR!

- self-improvement w/o reward bottleneck, incl meta-harness
(10:30 am, RSI workshop)

- emergent physical generalization, incl π0.7
(11:40 am, multimodal workshop)

- RL for robustness
(1:45 pm, CAO workshop)

I'll give a talk about lifelong learning tmrw (Sun), 9:30 am (Brazil time) in the lifelong agents workshop, about how we can get robot foundation models to improve with RL: https://t.co/f2AXMDAjkq

Then at 11:30 am, I'll talk about how generative models can drive self-improvement
